title: Somalia Opposition Threatens Parallel Presidential Election Within One Month; Somali Future Council Calls 'Salvation Summit' as May 15 Deadline Looms
detail: With 21 days until President Hassan Sheikh Mohamud's presidential term expires on May 15, 2026, Somalia's opposition coalition escalated its pressure campaign on April 24, formally warning it will organize a parallel presidential election 'within one month' if Mohamud refuses inclusive talks. The Somali Future Council — an umbrella opposition body comprising former presidents, prime ministers, and civil society leaders — issued a 'salvation summit' call, urging all political stakeholders to convene an emergency national dialogue on a constitutional transition before the May 15 expiry. The opposition's ultimatum builds on weeks of escalating confrontation: Parliament's mandate expired April 14 without elections; senior FGS officials mass-defected to the opposition on April 21; and Puntland declared a 'constitutional vacuum' on April 17. The Future Council, backed by Jubbaland President Ahmed Madobe and Puntland, warned that if Mohamud continues to govern beyond May 15 without a political agreement, they would constitute an alternative federal executive — potentially triggering Somalia's worst governance crisis since 2012. UN Special Representative Catriona Laing and the African Union have urged dialogue, while the EU — which approved €75 million for AUSSOM on April 23 — has conditioned continued support on political stability. Al-Shabaab's April 19 seizure of Adan Yabal in Hiiraan, deliberately timed to exploit the governance vacuum, is being cited by opposition leaders as evidence that political paralysis directly enables security deterioration. IGAD is mediating but has made no visible progress.

media.caption: Somalia's opposition coalition threatens a parallel presidential election within one month as the Somali Future Council calls an emergency 'salvation summit' with 21 days to the May 15 presidential term expiry

title: SNA/Macawisley Counter-Offensive to Retake Adan Yabal Stalls in Mobilization Phase; Al-Shabaab Consolidates Hiiraan Hold Five Days After Seizure
detail: Five days after Al-Shabaab's April 19 seizure of Adan Yabal (Aadan Yabaal) in Hiiraan Region — in which approximately 100 militants routed SNA forces and killed 8 civilians — the Somali National Army and Macawisley clan militia counter-offensive mandated by President Mohamud remains in mobilization phase as of April 24, 2026. Security analysts and Somali observers note that Al-Shabaab has had five days to reinforce the town, set IED belts on approach routes, and establish defensive positions in the former SNA outposts. The delay is attributed to logistical challenges (difficult terrain, disrupted supply lines), the need to coordinate between SNA regular forces and clan militia elements, and the governance crisis: with parliament's mandate expired and the presidential term 21 days from expiry, command authority is disputed at multiple levels. AFRICOM-enabled air support and NISA intelligence operations are continuing in adjacent areas (including the April 22 Guulane raid that killed 33 militants in Middle Shabelle), but no confirmed SNA ground operation to retake Adan Yabal has been reported. Al-Shabaab's consolidation of Adan Yabal represents its most significant territorial gain in Hiiraan since the 2022 Macawisley offensive — a reversal of hard-won gains that took months of fighting to achieve. Security experts warn that every day of delay improves Al-Shabaab's defensive position and risks further setbacks on adjacent fronts.

sources.name: Combating Terrorism Center at West Point

media.caption: SNA and Macawisley counter-offensive to retake Adan Yabal stalls five days after Al-Shabaab's seizure of the strategic Hiiraan Region town, as mobilization challenges and the political crisis delay ground operations
